paper.title: A runaway T-Tauri star leaving an extended trail

review.summary: Paper contains one LaTeX table (Table 1: astrometric observation log) and one external URL (stilism.obspm.fr reddening maps). No other structured data tables, machine-readable files, or explicit data-availability resources found. The astrometric table records positions from 8 epochs (1953-2021) used to derive proper motion.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Log of astrometric observations used to derive proper motion of UJT-1. Records ICRS coordinates from 8 epochs spanning 1953-2021 from various telescopes/surveys.

columns.name: Source of image
columns.meaning: Telescope or survey providing the astrometric image

columns.name: Right Ascension
columns.meaning: Right Ascension offset in arcseconds from 308°07' (ICRS)

internal_tables.comments: Table footnote notes coordinates are in ICRS with 1-sigma errors. Sources: DSS1, DSS2, INT, Pan-STARRS1, Gaia DR3, UJT, NOT.

external_resources.description: Tridimensional reddening maps by Capitanio et al. (2017A&A...606A..65C), used to constrain distance to UJT-1 based on color excess

source_refs.context: However, according to the tridimensional reddening maps by \citet{2017A&A...606A..65C}\footnote{\url{https://stilism.obspm.fr}}, a distance of 2 kpc or higher is needed to reach the estimated color excess for the UJT-1 line of sight.

external_resources.evidence: Footnote URL in paper text referencing reddening maps used for distance estimation
